key_insight: Theoria verifies AI answers by rewriting them into auditable typed state transitions, so any hidden assumption becomes an explicit, checkable change instead of an opaque score.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical middle ground between full formal proof and free-form LLM judging, enabling higher-trust validation of expert-level math and reasoning outputs.
method: Rewrite candidate solutions into sequences of typed proof-state transitions.
Require every transition to be licensed by an explicit justification such as a citation, computation, or problem-given fact.
Enforce completeness of change so all differences between consecutive states must be accounted for.
evidence_signal: On HLE-Verified Gold, Theoria certifies 105/185 problems at 91.4% strict precision, and on 95 adversarial poisoned proofs it catches 94.7%, outperforming holistic judging especially on hidden premises and fabricated citations.
watch_item: Certification quality depends on the correctness and completeness of the rewrite/typing rules, so gaps in the state language or justification policy could allow subtle errors to slip through.
paper_title: Theoria: Rewrite-Acceptability Verification over Informal Reasoning States
